title: A ROS 2 Wrapper for Florence-2: Multi-Mode Local Vision-Language Inference for Robotic Systems

section_anchors.text: This article presents a ROS 2 wrapper for Florence-2 that exposes the model through three complementary interaction modes
Recent progress in foundation models has broadened the range of perception and reasoning capabilities that can be brought into robotic systems
The most relevant literature for this article lies at the intersection of three lines of work
The wrapper is centred on a single ROS 2 inference node that encapsulates model loading, image reception, prompt construction, inference, post-processing and publication of outputs
A key design choice in the wrapper is the exposure of three complementary interaction modes
The wrapper exposes a small but expressive ROS 2 interface surface
Florence-2 unifies multiple perception capabilities through prompt-based task tokens
The software is organised into two packages
The purpose of the evaluation is not to benchmark Florence-2 as a vision model against unrelated perception articles, but to validate the wrapper as a ROS 2 software component
The wrapper was functionally validated in its three supported modes
In addition to functional validation, we are also reporting end-to-end throughput measurements for the wrapper under continuous image processing
The current implementation has several limitations
This article has presented a ROS 2 wrapper for Florence-2 aimed at practical local deployment in robotic systems

notes: Manually validated against markdown_evaluation/pdfs/260401179v1.pdf on 2026-04-02 using pdftotext -layout and rendered-page inspection. Gold headings/anchors/assets were taken from the PDF itself, not benchmark outputs.
